description: Eval cases: skill should be invoked for explicit optimization/performance asks; description and body are aligned with PR #2 testing pattern.

cases.prompt: How do I optimize this MongoDB find? db.orders.find({ status: 'open' }).sort({ createdAt: -1 })
How should I index this aggregation $match so it uses an index? $match: { shopId: ObjectId('...'), date: { $gte: ISODate('2024-01-01') } }
Why is this query performing badly on my cluster? It's a find with filter on serialCode and sort by _id.
What are the slow queries on my cluster? Which should I fix first?
Can you fix my slow queries? I have indexes but things are still slow.
Write a find query for users where status is active and limit 10.

Can you optimize this aggregation pipeline? It's running slow: db.orders.aggregate([{ $lookup: { from: 'customers', localField: 'customerId', foreignField: '_id', as: 'customer' } }, { $match: { status: 'shipped' } }, { $group: { _id: '$customer.region', total: { $sum: '$amount' } } }])
How do I optimize a query on an array field? db.products.find({ tags: 'electronics', 'specs.features': { $all: ['wifi', 'bluetooth'] } }). The tags array has hundreds of values per document.

I want to make this query as fast as possible without reading documents. Query: db.users.find({ status: 'active', plan: 'premium' }, { status: 1, plan: 1, email: 1, _id: 0 }).sort({ email: 1 }). Can this be a covered query?
I ran explain on my query and got totalKeysExamined: 5000, nReturned: 50, totalDocsExamined: 5000. Is this good performance? What does this mean?

Why is my array query returning wrong results? db.orders.find({ 'items.sku': 'ABC123', 'items.quantity': { $gte: 5 } }). Sometimes it matches orders where ABC123 has quantity 2 and a different item has quantity 10.

My aggregation is timing out. It uses $unwind on an array with thousands of elements per document. Pipeline: [{ $unwind: '$events' }, { $match: { 'events.type': 'purchase' } }, { $group: { _id: '$userId', count: { $sum: 1 } } }]. How do I fix this?
